options.name: A) Single canonical Postgres (zakops.deals + related tables)
cons: Need clear schema for DataRoom + embeddings refs

options.name: B) Multiple stores with sync (event sourcing)
C) Keep current split

decision_set.recommendation: Choose A. Make Postgres the single source of truth for deal state; other stores are derived/indexed only.
migration: Add explicit columns for dataroom_path, external_ids, and canonical deal_id mapping in Postgres.
One-time migration: ingest deal_registry.json into Postgres with deterministic ID mapping.
Refactor all writers (email ingestion, executors, agent tools) to use Postgres APIs.
Deprecate and remove JSON registry + SQLite action store after parity tests pass.
verification: Create deal via API, email ingestion, and agent tool; verify single Postgres row and consistent deal_id.
Ripgrep shows no writes to deal_registry.json or SQLite action store.
Dashboards, agents, and backend all read from Postgres only.

decision_set.topic: Deal taxonomy + stage model (canonical stages and transitions)

options.name: A) Canonical stages from workflow.py with explicit transition rules
B) Use legacy deal_state_machine stages
C) Per-pipeline custom stages

decision_set.recommendation: Choose A. Align DB constraints and defaults to canonical stages and provide mapping for legacy labels.
migration: Update DB default stage from 'lead' to 'inbound'.
Add migration script to map legacy stages into canonical stages.
Remove/retire legacy deal_state_machine.py or mark as archived.
verification: DB constraint matches canonical list; no rows with non-canonical stage.
Transition endpoint rejects invalid transitions with 409.
UI and agent use the same canonical stage list.

options.name: A) Agent uses backend APIs only (no direct DB writes)
B) Agent writes to DB directly
C) Agent owns separate store and syncs

decision_set.recommendation: Choose A. Agent tools must call backend APIs and include correlation_id + HITL as required.
migration: Add create_deal tool that calls backend /api/deals with HITL approval.
Ensure all existing tools include correlation_id headers and actor info.
Block direct DB writes from agent service.
verification: Agent tool calls produce backend audit events with correlation_id.
No agent code references DB credentials or direct connections.

options.name: A) Event-driven ingestion (IMAP/SMTP -> queue -> triage -> quarantine -> deal update)
B) Cron-based polling (current pattern)
C) Manual upload only

decision_set.recommendation: Implement B immediately as a bridge, then evolve to A once canonical data path is stable.
migration: Point ingestion output to Postgres quarantine + deal tables.
Add idempotency on message_id and body_hash.
Attach emails and extracted artifacts to deal record; store files in DataRoom.
verification: Synthetic email appears as quarantine item and is linked to a deal after approval.
Attachments stored under deal folder; RAG indexing reflects new content.

options.name: A) Central RAG service with vector store keyed by deal_id
B) Agent-local embeddings
C) No embeddings

decision_set.recommendation: Choose A. Embed only canonical deal artifacts and store metadata in Postgres.
migration: Define indexing pipeline triggered on new emails/attachments/notes.
Add reindex_deal action and automated nightly refresh.
Expose RAG health endpoint and fail-closed behavior.
verification: search_deals returns expected hits for known content.
RAG health endpoint returns OK and indexing metrics.

options.name: A) Store approvals in backend DB with agent references
B) Keep approvals only in agent DB
C) Ephemeral approvals

decision_set.recommendation: Choose A. Mirror approvals into backend with correlation_id and deal_id.
migration: Add approvals table in backend and replicate current approvals on write.
Update approval APIs to emit audit events tied to deal_id and correlation_id.
verification: Approval records visible in backend with correlation_id.
Audit trail links agent action to deal_events.

options.name: A) OIDC/JWT for users + service tokens for internal services
B) Static API keys for UI
C) No auth

decision_set.recommendation: Choose A with local dev fallback. Enforce JWT on backend and attach user identity to deal_events.
migration: Add auth middleware to dashboard and backend.
Add RBAC policy for deal actions and admin actions.
Propagate user_id to audit and event tables.
verification: Audit logs include user_id for all writes.

phases.name: Phase 0: Stop the Bleeding & Observability Baseline
phases.objective: Establish security guardrails and end-to-end traceability; eliminate silent failures.
tasks: Add dashboard authentication (JWT/OIDC) and user identity propagation (ZK-ISSUE-0005).
Add correlation_id propagation dashboard -> backend -> agent and persist in deal_events + audit_log (ZK-ISSUE-0011).
Add structured logging + error surfacing and explicit health checks for RAG/email ingestion (ZK-ISSUE-0010).
Fix dashboard Zod handling to avoid silent drops; log schema mismatch (ZK-ISSUE-0018).
gates: All write endpoints require auth; correlation_id visible across logs; schema mismatches logged.
acceptance_criteria: Every write request contains correlation_id and user_id.
RAG/email health endpoints return explicit status (OK/FAIL).
evidence_required: Sample trace showing correlation_id across services

phases.name: Phase 1: Data Truth Unification
phases.objective: Eliminate split-brain persistence and unify actions/deals in Postgres.
tasks: Design canonical deal schema fields (including dataroom_path and external_ids).
Migrate JSON deal_registry into Postgres; keep id mapping and provenance (ZK-ISSUE-0001).
Refactor CreateDealFromEmailExecutor to write to Postgres and remove sys.path hack (ZK-ISSUE-0014).
Migrate actions engine to use Postgres zakops.actions; retire SQLite store (ZK-ISSUE-0008).
gates: Creating a deal via any path writes a single Postgres row; no JSON or SQLite writes.
acceptance_criteria: deal_registry.json not written by any process
Postgres is sole source for deals and actions
evidence_required: ripgrep results for legacy write paths

phases.name: Phase 2: Contract Alignment (UI, Backend, Agent)
phases.objective: Align API contracts and ensure UI/agent calls hit active backend endpoints.
tasks: Update dashboard to call /api/quarantine/{id}/process instead of legacy /resolve (ZK-ISSUE-0006).
Implement or align notes endpoint in orchestration API and update UI (ZK-ISSUE-0012).
Add agent create_deal tool with HITL and backend validation (ZK-ISSUE-0009).
Generate and consume OpenAPI client to reduce schema drift; harden Zod schemas (ZK-ISSUE-0018).
gates: No 404s for UI core flows; agent tool contract tested against backend.
acceptance_criteria: Agent create_deal works only with approval

phases.name: Phase 3: Deal Lifecycle Correctness
phases.objective: Make quarantine, stages, and transitions correct and deterministic.
tasks: Align stage taxonomy across DB, backend workflow, UI, and agent; fix default stage (ZK-ISSUE-0007).
Implement atomic quarantine approval -> deal creation/linking with idempotency (ZK-ISSUE-0003).
Add duplicate deal detection (unique constraint or semantic match) (ZK-ISSUE-0016).
Add archive/restore endpoints in orchestration API (ZK-ISSUE-0022).
gates: Approving quarantine creates a deal; stage transitions validated; archive/restore works.

phases.name: Phase 4: Deal Knowledge System (Email, DataRoom, RAG, Actions)
phases.objective: Turn each deal into a living knowledge object with email/attachments/RAG integration.
tasks: Re-enable email ingestion targeting Postgres quarantine/deals and attach to deals (ZK-ISSUE-0002).
Create DataRoom folder scaffolding on deal creation; store path in DB (ZK-ISSUE-0004).
Wire action executors to UI and workflows with tracking (ZK-ISSUE-0019).
Implement /api/actions/capabilities and /metrics from registry (ZK-ISSUE-0013).
Implement RAG indexing pipeline and validate search_deals (ZK-ISSUE-0010).
Add scheduling/reminders framework for follow-ups and SLAs (ZK-ISSUE-0021).
gates: Email -> quarantine -> deal update -> RAG searchable; DataRoom folders created.
acceptance_criteria: New emails appear under correct deal
Deal folder exists for new deal

phases.name: Phase 5: Hardening & Real-time UX
phases.objective: Operational hardening, expiry, retention, and realtime updates.
tasks: Add approval expiry background job and metrics (ZK-ISSUE-0015).
Define and implement retention/cleanup policy for archived/junk deals (ZK-ISSUE-0017).
Implement SSE/WebSocket stream for realtime updates (ZK-ISSUE-0020).
gates: Expired approvals auto-transition; UI updates via SSE without polling.
acceptance_criteria: Pending approvals older than TTL become expired
Archived deals cleaned according to policy

phases.name: Phase 6: Legacy Decommission & Final Proof
phases.objective: Remove legacy services, paths, and dead endpoints; prove no references remain.
tasks: Remove deal_lifecycle API and legacy endpoints (/resolve, /note, /archive) after replacements exist.
Delete or archive /home/zaks/scripts legacy registry/state machine modules.
Remove JSON deal_registry and SQLite action DB; add CI guard to prevent reintroduction.
Confirm no sys.path hacks or direct legacy imports exist.
gates: rg finds no legacy references; services only expose canonical endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: No references to deal_registry.json or scripts path

tasks: Migrate JSON registry to Postgres
Remove split-brain writers
verification: Create deal via all paths -> single Postgres row

tasks: Enable ingestion pipeline
Write to Postgres quarantine/deal tables
Approve quarantine -> create deal
Idempotency guard
verification: Approve item yields new deal row
coverage_matrix.done: Single action creates deal and links quarantine

tasks: Create DataRoom folders on deal creation
coverage_matrix.done: Deal has folder and path stored

tasks: Add dashboard auth
Update UI to /process
Align stage taxonomy
Fix DB default
Migrate actions engine to Postgres
Add create_deal agent tool with HITL
coverage_matrix.done: Agent can create deals via backend

tasks: RAG health checks
Index deals
Add correlation_id propagation
verification: Same correlation_id appears in agent + backend logs

tasks: Add notes endpoint in orchestration or update UI
Implement /actions/capabilities and /metrics
Remove sys.path hack and legacy imports
Add approval expiry background job
Add duplicate detection
Implement retention/cleanup policy
Zod passthrough + schema sync
Wire action executors into workflows
Implement SSE/WebSocket updates
Add scheduling/reminders
Add archive/restore endpoints

prioritized_backlog.verification: Single Postgres row created from all paths

prioritized_backlog.outcome: Agent can create deals with HITL

prioritized_backlog.verification: RAG health OK + search_deals returns results
